questions.prompt: How do you make a table append-only via RLS?

choices.text: Define an INSERT policy (with appropriate WITH CHECK to validate ownership/team) and optionally a SELECT policy. OMIT UPDATE and DELETE policies entirely. With RLS enabled and no UPDATE/DELETE policies, those operations are denied for non-service-role users. The table accepts new rows but no one can modify or remove them — audit trails, transaction history, immutable logs. Database-enforced immutability.

questions.explanation: Omit UPDATE/DELETE policies; RLS denies them by default.

questions.prompt: Building Google-Docs-style sharing — owner full access, specific other users granted view/edit. RLS approach?

choices.text: Create a `document_shares` join table with (document_id, shared_with_user_id, permission). On the documents table: 'Owner full access' policy using `owner_id = auth.uid()`; 'Shared users can view' policy using EXISTS check on document_shares; 'Shared editors can update' policy with permission = 'edit' check. Index document_shares(shared_with_user_id, document_id) for fast policy lookups. Composable, follows access changes automatically as you add/remove rows from the share table.

questions.explanation: Join table + EXISTS check in policies. Clean and indexable.
